title: centaurea ninae
intro: Centaurea ninae, also known as Centaurea cyanus 'Nina' or Cornflower, is a perennial plant species in the daisy family (Asteraceae) that is native to the Mediterranean region. The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and inflammation, due to its antispasmodic, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory properties. Consuming Centaurea ninae tea or infusions may provide health benefits such as alleviating digestive discomfort, reducing fever, and acting as a natural antihistamine. Herbal preparations of Centaurea ninae include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
